description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with an automation stage containing five jobs: auto_deploy:check_package, auto_deploy:check_production, auto_deploy:cleanup, auto_deploy:deploy_latest, and auto_deploy:deploy_version. Each job runs in the automation stage using a Ruby environment with Bundler (via .with-bundle or .common-job-setup) and executes a specific rake task: `bundle exec rake 'auto_deploy:check_package'`, `bundle exec rake 'auto_deploy:check_production'`, `bundle exec rake 'auto_deploy:cleanup'`, `bundle exec rake 'auto_deploy:deploy_latest'`, and `bundle exec rake "auto_deploy:deploy_version[$VERSION,$FORCE_DEPLOY_VERSION]"` respectively. The check_package, cleanup, deploy_latest, and deploy_version jobs are triggered when their corresponding variables ($CHECK_PACKAGE, $AUTO_DEPLOY_CLEANUP, $AUTO_DEPLOY_DEPLOY_LATEST, $AUTO_DEPLOY_DEPLOY_VERSION) are set to "true" and run always, while check_production runs when $CHECK_PRODUCTION is "true" and may fail with exit code 10 without failing the pipeline. The check_production job caches the files SLACK_DEPLOYMENT_MESSAGE_TS and vendor/ruby under the key deployment_notification_ts, and is tagged with the "release" runner; the deploy_latest job uses a resource_group named auto-deploy-deploy-latest to prevent concurrent runs. No explicit Docker image or services are defined, so the pipeline inherits the project's default image.
